sourcesContent:
/** * About / Creator page. Polished SaaS-style story page with clear navigation * (breadcrumb, Home button, Back button, Escape shortcut) so users are never * stranded. */ import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; import { useNavigate, Link } from 'react-router-dom'; import { motion } from 'framer-motion'; import { Sparkles, Code2, Clock, Bug, Wand2, MessageCircle, Download, Globe2, Palette, Database, Shield, HeartHandshake, Github, Linkedin, Instagram, ArrowLeft, Home as HomeIcon, ChevronRight, } from 'lucide-react'; import { Card } from '../shared/ui'; const benefits = [ { icon: , title: 'Plain-English explanations', text: 'Beginner-friendly walkthroughs of any snippet.' }, { icon: , title: 'Line-by-line walkthroughs', text: 'Every meaningful statement gets its own comment.' }, { icon: , title: 'Time and space complexity', text: 'Correct, code-specific Big-O with reasoning.' }, { icon: , title: 'Bug and code-smell detection', text: 'Flagged as part of Suggested Improvements.' }, { icon: , title: 'Best-practice suggestions', text: 'Actionable, grounded in your identifiers.' }, { icon: , title: 'AI follow-up Q and A', text: 'Ask more, in context, about the same code.' }, { icon: , title: 'Markdown and PDF export', text: 'Save any analysis for later or share it.' }, { icon: , title: 'Multi-language support', text: 'Python and JavaScript first-class; more supported.' }, { icon: , title: 'Modern responsive UI', text: 'Design-system-accurate on every screen size.' }, { icon: , title: 'Persistent local history', text: 'Your past analyses, saved on your device.' }, { icon: , title: 'Privacy-first design', text: 'History never leaves your browser.' }, { icon: , title: 'Built with care', text: 'Small feature set, executed properly.' }, ]; const fadeStagger = (i: number) => ({ initial: { opacity: 0, y: 12 }, whileInView: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }, viewport: { once: true, margin: '-40px' }, transition: { duration: 0.5, delay: i * 0.04, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] as const }, }); export const AboutPage: React.FC = () => { const navigate = useNavigate(); // Escape closes the About page and returns Home. This is a small // accessibility polish so users can bail out without hunting for a button. useEffect(() => { const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { if (e.key === 'Escape') navigate('/'); }; window.addEventListener('keydown', handler); return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handler); }, [navigate]); return (
{/* Breadcrumb + local navigation row */}
Home About
Home
Press Esc to return home
{/* Hero */}
About CodeExplain CodeExplain was built to bridge the gap between reading code and truly understanding it, turning every snippet into a structured, teachable artifact.
{/* Story */}

The problem

Many beginners struggle to understand existing source code because most AI tools simply explain what the code does without teaching why it works. Documentation is uneven, tutorials skip lines, and large codebases feel opaque.

Why I built this

As a computer-science student, I often found that understanding existing{' '} code was harder than writing new code. CodeExplain combines AI-powered explanations with a clean, structured UI so the learning curve gets shorter and your programming intuition gets sharper.

{/* Benefits grid */}
Key benefits

Everything a code learner actually needs

{benefits.map((b, i) => (
{b.icon}
{b.title}

{b.text}

 ))}
{/* Privacy note */}

Privacy note

Your analysis history is stored locally in your browser. Nothing is uploaded or saved permanently unless required for AI processing. You remain in control of your data and can clear everything at any time from the History drawer.

{/* Creator card */}

); };
